content.full_text:
July 28, 1976

Mr. Thomas L. Saybolt  
Staff Attorney  
Ford Motor Company  
The American Road  
Dearborn, Michigan 48121

Dear Mr. Saybolt:

With regard to your request for information about campaign disclosure requirements applicable to corporate and union political action committees, I am sending to you, at the suggestion of Robert Naylor, a copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18423. This regulation indicates that the payment of salary, reimbursement for personal expenses, or other compensation by an employer to an employee who spends less than 10 percent of his compensated time in any one month rendering services for political purposes is not a campaign contribution or expenditure. The regulation provides guidelines as to when such activity is a reportable contribution or expenditure.

Also enclosed for your information are copies of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225 regarding the reporting of contributions and expenditures. Corporations should report all administrative or overhead costs that are directly attributable to their political activity but are not required to report regular on-going business overhead which would be incurred in similar amounts regardless of their political activity.

I hope this additional information is of assistance. If you have any questions, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,

NAN HAMBLETON  
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak  
Enclosures  

cc: Mr. Robert Naylor  
bcc: Jeanne
